nameMeanings.name: Idle
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh: from the personal name Ith(a)el, Old Welsh Iudhail ‘bountiful lord’. Compare Ethell , Bithell .

variants.name: Idell
meanings: English: habitational name from Idle in Calverley, Yorkshire, which was named in Old English from īdel ‘idle, unused’, probably denoting an area of uncultivated land.

English: nickname from Middle English idel ‘idle, lazy’. Alternatively, from an unrecorded Middle English female personal name Idele (Old French Idele, ancient Germanic Idela), a pet form of Ide (Old French Ide; see Ide ).
Jewish (Ashkenazic): from the Yiddish personal name Idl, a pet form of Jude .

en.lifeSummary: Idle married Idle before 1501, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter.
